channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Prompt Engineering
channel.description: Latest Prompt Engineering news in Context Engineering

item.title: HumorGen: Cognitive Synergy for Humor Generation in Large Language Models via Persona-Based Distillation
item.description: arXiv:2604.09629v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Humor generation poses a significant challenge for Large Language Models (LLMs), because their standard training objective - predicting the most likely next word - inherently conflicts with the surprise and incongruity needed for comedy. To bridge thi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, prompt, model, framework, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Generating High Quality Synthetic Data for Dutch Medical Conversations
item.description: arXiv:2604.09645v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Medical conversations offer insights into clinical communication often absent from Electronic Health Records. However, developing reliable clinical Natural Language Processing (NLP) models is hampered by the scarcity of domain-specific datasets, as cl...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompting, arxiv, analysis, prompt, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Spoiler Alert: Narrative Forecasting as a Metric for Tension in LLM Storytelling
item.description: arXiv:2604.09854v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLMs have so far failed both to generate consistently compelling stories and to recognize this failure--on the leading creative-writing benchmark (EQ-Bench), LLM judges rank zero-shot AI stories above New Yorker short stories, a gold standard for lite...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> template, arxiv, analysis, model, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: STaR-DRO: Stateful Tsallis Reweighting for Group-Robust Structured Prediction
item.description: arXiv:2604.09737v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Structured prediction requires models to generate ontology-constrained labels, grounded evidence, and valid structure under ambiguity, label skew, and heterogeneous group difficulty. We present a two-part framework for controllable inference and robus...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompting, arxiv, analysis, prompt, in-context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Context-Engineering - "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.
item.description: "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt, context, context window, prompt engineering | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Claude-Code-Everything-You-Need-to-Know - The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.
item.description: The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt, tool, prompt engineering, step-by-step, example | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: AlphaCodium - Official implementation for the paper: "Code Generation with AlphaCodium: From Prompt Engineering to Flow Engineering""
item.description: Official implementation for the paper: "Code Generation with AlphaCodium: From Prompt Engineering to Flow Engineering""<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt, paper, prompt engineering | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: optillm - Optimizing inference proxy for LLMs
item.description: Optimizing inference proxy for LLMs<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
